item.title: FT8 Launch
item.dc:creator: Rob Bryan
item.description:
Confession

I worked on Digital Signal Processing for almost 6 weeks without learning that FT8 exists.

Challenge

I’m going to try to write my own FT8 demodulator in 30 days with:

  • AI in strict socratic mode
  • Posts of status by 7:00 AM (EDT) on Monday, Thursday, and Saturday.

My solution won’t rely on any part of the working FT8 demodulator in WSJT-X.

There’s at least one public python solution. I’ve seen it. I won’t look at it again during the challenge.

Note

If I find any other solutions, I’ll avert my eyes immediately.

What does success look like?

At the outset, I’ll make a 20-minute reference recording of audio on the main JT8 frequency, 14.074 MHz and demodulate that with WSJT-X.

To assess my progress, I’ll demodulate the same audio with my code and compare the results.

item.title: Digital Signal Processing
item.dc:creator: Rob Bryan
item.description:
Digital signal processing (DSP) is the study of analyzing, modifying, and synthesizing signals, where signals are any observable change in a quantity over space or time.

Signals do not have to carry information, but they can, and DSP is way more fun when they do.

These are my primary DSP resources:

  • Wikipedia : Digital signal processing
  • Wikipedia : Signal
  • Think DSP by Allen Downey
  • DSP First 2e from Georgia Tech
  • https://jackschaedler.github.io/
  • The Scientist and Engineer’s Guide to Digital Signal Processing by Steven Smith
  • Signal Processing with SciPy
  • Understanding Digital Signal Processing by Richard Lyons
